text.title: Python Domain Map and Framework Routing
text.summary: Python domain classification (web/data/ML/automation/embedded) and next-methodology routing table.

rule.statement: Classify the project's primary domain before loading any framework-specific methodology. Use imports and dependency list as signals.
rule.rationale: Python spans incompatible domains. Loading the wrong methodology produces irrelevant patterns and wastes context.

list.item: web-django — imports: django, rest_framework, drf. Admin panel, ORM, full-stack → django-models/, django-pytest/
web-fastapi — imports: fastapi, pydantic, sqlalchemy. Async API, ML serving → python-fastapi/
web-flask — imports: flask. Prototype, internal tool → python-web-frameworks/ for decision, then Flask specifics
data-science — imports: pandas, polars, numpy, jupyter. → data science methodologies
ml — imports: torch, tensorflow, sklearn, transformers, langchain. → ml-engineer skill
automation — imports: airflow, prefect, selenium, ansible. → automation methodologies
embedded/system — Rust + PyO3, Cython, cffi. → performance methodologies

rule.statement: For web projects: if framework is already chosen, load only the framework-specific methodology. Load python-web-frameworks only when the choice is still open.
rule.rationale: python-web-frameworks is a routing layer — it adds overhead when the choice is already made.

section.title: Python Domains Overview (2025-2026)
section.p: Web: FastAPI dominates new API projects; Django remains strong for admin-heavy apps. Flask for prototypes and internal tools.
Data: pandas for manipulation; polars gaining for large datasets (Rust performance); NumPy for numeric computing.
ML/AI: PyTorch for research; TensorFlow for production mobile; scikit-learn for classical ML; Hugging Face for NLP; pydantic-ai for type-safe LLM interactions.
Automation: Apache Airflow for workflow orchestration; Prefect for modern workflow management; Selenium/PyAutoGUI for UI automation.
